channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Indian Air Force Seeks Hydrogen-Powered Autonomous Airship for Persistent ISR and High-Altitude Operations
item.description: In a significant push toward next-generation aerospace capabilities, the Indian Air Force (IAF) has initiated a program to develop a hydrogen-powered, pilotless “Medium Altitude Heavy Lift Airship” designed for long-endurance intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) missions. The initiative reflects a broader shift toward persistent aerial platforms capable of remaining airborne for extended durations while carrying substantial payloads for multi-domain operations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: MH-6 Little Bird CSAR Mission in Iran Renews Spotlight on Helicopter that was Once Offered to India
item.description: The recent reports of MH-6 Little Bird helicopters executing a high-risk Combat Search and Rescue (CSAR) mission deep inside Iranian mountainous terrain have once again brought global attention to one of the most specialized rotary-wing platforms in modern warfare—an aircraft that was quietly offered to India in the aftermath of the 2019 Balakot air strikes but never inducted.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army’s Rs 250 Crore FECA Fund Could Transform Defence Startups and Fast-Track Military Innovation
item.description: India’s long-standing challenge of translating defence startup innovations into actual military deployments may finally see a structural breakthrough, as the Indian Army pushes for the creation of a dedicated Field Exploitation and Capability Acceleration (FECA) fund. Valued at Rs 250 crore annually per Service Headquarters, the proposal—currently under consideration by the Ministry of Defence—aims to address a systemic bottleneck that has stalled numerous promising technologies at the trial stage.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NewSpace’s Sheshnag20 Swarming Loitering Munition Marks India’s Entry into AI-Enabled Drone Saturation Warfare
item.description: India’s rapidly evolving drone warfare ecosystem has taken a significant leap forward with NewSpace Research & Technologies signaling readiness to offer its Sheshnag20 system to the Indian Army for trials. Positioned as the country’s first indigenously designed canister-launched swarming loitering munition in its class, the Sheshnag20 represents a shift from standalone drone operations to coordinated, AI-driven swarm warfare tailored for modern battlefields.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From Hyping Jet Losses in Operation Sindoor to Celebrating Pilot Rescue in Iran: The Selective Narrative Around Mission Success and Material Costs
item.description: In the aftermath of India’s Operation Sindoor — the May 2025 military campaign involving precision strikes on nine terrorist sites and reported follow-on actions against Pakistani airbases — US President Donald Trump repeatedly emphasized aircraft losses. He cited figures ranging from five to eleven “very expensive jets” shot down during the India-Pakistan hostilities, framing his role as the decisive force that halted escalation through threats of massive trade tariffs. This focus on attrition numbers, rather than the operational objectives of dismantling terror infrastructure, drew sharp criticism in India as a calculated tactic. Many viewed it as an attempt to weaken India’s negotiating position on trade issues, including opening sensitive sectors like agriculture, by amplifying perceived vulnerabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO 2.0 and the Laser Frontier: How India’s Directed-Energy Weapons Are Rewriting Anti-Drone and Missile Defence Rules
item.description: Few developments in Indian defence R&D have been as under-reported yet strategically consequential as the rapid maturation of directed-energy systems. The April 2025 field trial of the Mk-II(A) Laser-Directed Energy Weapon at Kurnool marked a generational leap: a 30-kilowatt vehicle-mounted system demonstrated simultaneous engagement of fixed-wing UAVs, swarm formations, and surveillance sensors at ranges previously considered aspirational. By damaging structural components and frying electronics at the speed of light, the test placed India among the world’s elite operators of high-energy laser technology, joining a select group that had previously dominated this domain.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From EL/M-2052 to GaN Uttam AESA—How India Is Evolving Tejas Mk1A Radar Strategy
item.description: India’s roadmap for equipping the Tejas Mk1A with an indigenous AESA radar has undergone a notable evolution, reflecting both technological maturation and program timelines. What began as a phased induction of the Uttam AESA radar alongside imported systems has now shifted toward a more decisive transition, with the second batch of aircraft expected to feature next-generation Gallium Nitride (GaN) based radar technology.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Cong keeps singing to Pak’s tunes, compromising nation’s security: PM Modi
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Monday said while his government carried out ‘Operation Sindoor’ on terror camps in Pakistan, the Congress continued to “sing to the tune of” the neighbouring country. Addressing an election rally in Assam’s Barpeta district, he also accused the opposition party of taking a myopic view on development, while asserting that the BJP focuses on long-term growth. “The party always took short-term measures so that they could indulge in corruption. But the BJP looks far ahead while taking decisions for all-around development of all sections of society,” Modi said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Attempt to divert attention’: BJP on Pak Minister Khwaja Asif’s remark
item.description: A day after Pakistan Defence Minister Khawaja Asif warning of a retaliatory strike on Kolkata in the event of any “future misadventure” by India has triggered strong reactions across India’s political spectrum, with leaders condemning the remarks and urging restraint.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Prahaar Pressure: Desperate ISI Pushes Khalistani Groups For Strikes In North India
item.description: A deliberate strategy to keep outfits such as the Lashkar-e-Taiba quiet for the time being is being orchestrated by the ISI. A major push is being made to promote the Khalistani terror groups, with the targets being Punjab and Delhi, an official said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Mamata demands PM’s resignation, questions his silence on Pak’s ‘threat’ to strike Kolkata
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee on Monday asked why Prime Minister Narendra Modi did not raise Pakistan’s threat to attack Kolkata, during his poll rallies in West Bengal a day earlier. “You (PM) target Bengal during election rallies; but when Pakistan talks of attacking Bengal, you do not utter a word. You should resign,” she said at a poll rally here in Nadia district.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: CRPF’s CoBra jawan injured in IED explosion in J’khand’s West Singhbhum
item.description: A jawan of CRPF’s CoBRA battalion was injured when an IED planted by CPI (Maoists) exploded in Jharkhand’s West Singhbhum district, a police officer said on Monday. The incident occurred when a joint anti-Maoist operation was going on in Saranda forest under the Chotanagra police station limit, Superintendent of Police Amit Renu told PTI.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Remains of seafarer killed in drone boat attack reach Mumbai
item.description: The mortal remains of 25-year-old Indian seafarer Dixit Solanki reached Mumbai on Sunday, weeks after he was killed in a suspected drone boat attack off the coast of Oman during the West Asia conflict. Solanki, who was working aboard an oil tanker, died on March 4 when an explosive-laden drone boat struck the vessel in the Arabian Sea.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Air India suspends flights to Israel till May 31
item.description: Amid the ongoing war in West Asia, Air India has suspended its flights to Israel till May 31. An Air India executive confirmed to PTI that the airline has suspended flights on the New Delhi-Tel Aviv route till May 31.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Search operation launched after suspicious movement in J-K’s Samba
item.description: Security forces on Sunday launched a search operation at a village in Samba district of Jammu and Kashmir following reports of movement of three suspicious individuals, officials said. A resident of Bedh Khad in the Darui area reported spotting three unidentified persons with one carrying a heavy backpack, moving along a dried-up stream under the cover of darkness.
item.dc:creator: admin
